name: Bank Of Brookfield - Purdin, National Association
description_short: Independent community bank serving north central Missouri since 1894 with branches in Brookfield, Purdin, Meadville, and Winigan offering traditional and electronic banking products.
description_long: Bank of Brookfield-Purdin is an independent, locally-owned and locally-managed financial institution that has served its communities since 1894. The bank operates four branch locations across north central Missouri: Brookfield and Meadville in Linn County, and Purdin and Winigan in Sullivan County. All branches are staffed by community members who live in the areas they serve, emphasizing personal relationships and local decision-making. The bank positions itself as a bridge between small community banking and larger institutional services. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of traditional banking products and services including checking and savings accounts, CDs and IRAs, home loans, and various loan products. They provide modern conveniences such as online banking with bill pay features, mobile apps with new functionality, ATM and debit card services, check reordering, and e-statements. Their product range appears comparable to larger regional banks while maintaining a community-focused approach to service delivery. What distinguishes Bank of Brookfield-Purdin is their explicit commitment to remaining independent, locally owned, and community-minded while offering electronic banking options typically associated with larger institutions. Their motto—"Small enough to know you, large enough to serve you, and strong enough to protect you"—reflects their positioning as a hybrid: maintaining personal relationships while delivering modern banking technology and services. The bank emphasizes staffing with individuals committed to making positive differences in their communities beyond just financial services. Bank of Brookfield-Purdin is well-suited for residents of their service area seeking traditional community banking with modern conveniences. However, customers outside their four-branch service area would need to rely entirely on digital channels. The bank does not appear to offer specialized services like credit cards, wealth management, or investment advisory, focusing instead on foundational banking products. Their lack of broader geographic presence and service specialization may limit appeal for customers seeking comprehensive financial solutions or those living outside their Missouri footprint.
pros: Independently owned and locally managed with staff living in the communities they serve
Operating continuously since 1894, demonstrating stability and long-term community commitment
Offers modern digital banking including online banking, bill pay, and mobile apps
Multiple branch locations across four communities (Brookfield, Purdin, Meadville, Winigan)
Provides full-service banking including checking, savings, CDs, IRAs, and home loans
E-statement option for convenient and secure statement delivery
Personalized service with stated emphasis on knowing customers by name
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to four small Missouri towns, no online-only account opening for out-of-area customers mentioned
No credit card products offered, limiting rewards and credit-building opportunities
Website content does not mention investment, wealth management, or business banking services beyond basic small business needs
No indication of competitive rate information, APY disclosures, or fee structure details on website
Mobile and online banking features appear basic compared to regional and national bank offerings
best_for: Residents of Brookfield, Purdin, Meadville, or Winigan seeking personalized community banking with modern conveniences
Customers prioritizing local decision-making and personal relationships over national brand banking
Small business owners in their service area seeking basic business checking and lending
diagnosis: Bank of Brookfield-Purdin is best for residents in their four-community service area in north central Missouri who value personal relationships and local decision-making in banking. The main caveat is that their geographic limitation and lack of specialized services (credit cards, investment products, wealth management) make them unsuitable for customers outside their footprint or those seeking comprehensive financial solutions beyond basic banking products.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: Independent bank serving north central Missouri since 1894 with branches in Brookfield, Purdin, Meadville, and Winigan offering traditional banking.
fdic_official_name: Bank of Brookfield - Purdin, National Association
